messages.text:
Eric, both accomplished what needs to be done…we might want to amend the operating agreement in case of a sale…having unanimous votes can be an issue, perhaps a super majority (assume 6 of 8 votes) would work. We might want to add an offer to “reacquire” shares of a manager no longer wanting ownership in SoyLei, we’d have to determine a value at the time. On the Chairman role, I would suggest a super majority as well for termination. Option shares scheme is acceptable. 

The basics are covered…the NDA and NC are long and boilerplate -no issue, arbitration is good, Iowa jurisdiction OK…so, other then filing in name and address, it looks good…hate to spend lawyers.
What any good lawyer will tell you is “whatever man can write, man can unwrite “.
…agreements are only as good as the people making them…if we where all together it would be a hand shake.

Great, if the Chairman agreement is good as is, please modify name / address as needed.

This version of the amendment uses supermajorities for the "big" changes.

If this all looks good, I will share with the other owners for comment. If they don't have any strong objections to executing without review, I'm fine with that too.

attachments.name: 2024-04-01 AMENDMENT TO THE OPERATING AGREEMENT - SoyLei Innovations.docx
